Source.Name: brightnessctl
Source.Summary: A program to read and control device brightness
Source.Description:
brightnessctl is a lightweight command-line utility to control device brightness on Linux. It can control backlights (screen brightness) and LEDs without requiring sudo/root privileges when properly configured with udev rules or systemd-logind API.
Features: - Control screen brightness from the command line - Support for keyboard backlights and LEDs - Can be used without root privileges with proper udev rules - Supports both absolute and relative brightness changes - Works with systemd-logind for permission management

Package.Name: brightnessctl
Package.Summary: A program to read and control device brightness
Package.Description:
brightnessctl is a lightweight command-line utility to control device brightness on Linux. It can control backlights (screen brightness) and LEDs without requiring sudo/root privileges when properly configured with udev rules or systemd-logind API.
Features: - Control screen brightness from the command line - Support for keyboard backlights and LEDs - Can be used without root privileges with proper udev rules - Supports both absolute and relative brightness changes - Works with systemd-logind for permission management
